name: AdminJS Node.js Admin Panel Framework
what_it_does: Open-source Node.js admin panel framework — enabling backend developers to generate customizable admin interfaces over existing databases (MongoDB via Mongoose, PostgreSQL via Sequelize/TypeORM/Prisma, MySQL, etc.) with automatic CRUD operations, custom actions, dashboard, role-based permissions, and hooks for business logic, without building admin UIs from scratch.
use_cases: Generating admin CRUD panels over MongoDB or PostgreSQL via AdminJS for internal operations
Adding custom admin actions via AdminJS API hooks for operational workflow automation
Configuring AdminJS role-based access via API for team permission management
Customizing AdminJS dashboard with custom components via framework API for data visualization
Building white-labeled admin panels via AdminJS for SaaS platform internal management
not_for: Customer-facing applications (AdminJS is internal admin panels only)
Non-Node.js backends (AdminJS is JavaScript/TypeScript only — use Forest Admin for others)
Teams without Node.js/React development resources for setup and customization
best_when: A Node.js development team needs a fully customizable, self-hosted admin panel for their database — where AdminJS generates the UI from the schema, with hooks for custom validation, actions, and display components.
avoid_when: You're using non-Node.js backend (use Forest Admin), need managed cloud admin UI (use Retool), or are customer-facing.

auth.notes: Not a cloud API — AdminJS is a self-hosted Node.js framework. Authentication via AdminJS authentication provider (custom or built-in). Custom auth handler in Node.js code. Password-based auth with session cookies. OAuth2 via custom auth provider (Google, GitHub, etc.). Role-based access via canInvokeAction, canEditResource hooks. No webhooks — uses internal before/after hooks in Node.js. Deployed as part of your Express/Fastify/NestJS server.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source (MIT license) — completely free self-hosted
pricing.paid_tiers: AdminJS Cloud Pro (coming); Support plans via SoftwareBrothers
pricing.notes: Polish company (Warsaw). Created by SoftwareBrothers. MIT license. GitHub: SoftwareBrothers/adminjs (8,000+ stars). TypeScript + React. ORM adapters: Mongoose (MongoDB), Sequelize (PostgreSQL/MySQL), TypeORM, Prisma, MikroORM. HTTP framework adapters: Express, Fastify, Hapi, NestJS, Koa. Originally named admin-bro (renamed AdminJS 2021). Used by startups building SaaS products needing internal admin. Customizable React components. Docker-ready. Community plugins for auth (Google OAuth, password). AWS Cognito auth adapter available.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Framework (not API) — no external API endpoints. Self-hosted Node.js. TypeScript support. ORM adapters for multiple databases. Not an external REST API — embeds in your Express/Fastify server. For agents: interact via the underlying database, not AdminJS endpoints. Good documentation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: CRUD operations via AdminJS use the underlying ORM — idempotency matches ORM behavior. Reads are fully idempotent. Custom actions require custom idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: AdminJS is a FRAMEWORK, not an API service — no external REST API endpoints to call
Agents should interact with the underlying database directly, not AdminJS UI endpoints
Renamed from admin-bro — documentation may reference old name
Node.js/TypeScript expertise required for setup and customization
Authentication must be implemented — AdminJS doesn't include auth out-of-box

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on server config. User-controlled data residency. Custom auth required — no built-in auth provider (security responsibility on developer). Role-based access via canInvokeAction hooks. Open source MIT — auditable. TypeScript for type safety. User responsible for server security and auth implementation. Database credentials managed by developer.
